name: For Loop
description: Generates a range of indices and outputs them as a list. Fires exec_out once after all indices are ready.
python_code:
async def execute(self, inputs):
    try: start = int(inputs.get('start', 0))
    except: start = 0
    try: end = int(inputs.get('end', 10))
    except: end = 10
    try: step = int(inputs.get('step', 1))
    except: step = 1
    if step == 0: step = 1

    indices = list(range(start, end, step))
    self.log_info(f'For loop: {len(indices)} iteration(s) ({start} to {end} step {step})')

    last_index = indices[-1] if indices else 0
    await self.set_output('indices', indices)
    await self.set_output('current_index', last_index)

    # Fire exec_out ONCE after indices are ready so downstream nodes
    # (e.g. loop_body) receive the full list before iterating.
    await self.set_output('exec_out', True)

    return {'indices': indices, 'current_index': last_index}
